instruction:
The Question Paper contains three sections.
 Section A has 24 questions. Attempt any 20 questions.
 Section B has 24 questions. Attempt any 20 questions.
 Section C has 12 questions. Attempt any 10 questions.
 All questions carry equal marks.
 There is no negative marking.

questions.question: Which serve as quick referral systems in taxonomical studies?
questions.question_raw: Which serve as quick referral systems in taxonomical studies?
questions.explanation: Museums have collections of preserved plant and animal specimens for study and reference. Manuals are useful in providing information for the identification of names of species found in an area. Monographs contain information on anyone's taxon. Hence, the correct option is Herbarium.

questions.question: Cytoskeleton is made of.
questions.question_raw: Cytoskeleton is made of.
questions.explanation: Cytoskeleton is a series of intercellular proteins that help a cell with shape, support andmovement. So, cytoskeleton is made up of proteinaceous filaments.

questions.question: Agar is obtained from_______.
questions.question_raw: Agar is obtained from_______.
questions.explanation: Agar, one of the commercial products obtained from Gelidium are used to grow microbes and in preparations of ice-creams and jellies.

questions.question: The cross-section of body of an invertebrate is given below. Identify the animal which has this body plan:
questions.question_raw: The cross-section of body of an invertebrate is given below. Identify the animal which has this body plan:
questions.explanation: The given diagram is cross section of roundworm, an invertebrate that possesses coelom and centrally located alimentary canal.

questions.question: The dye plant belonging to family Fabaceae is______.
questions.question_raw: The dye plant belonging to family Fabaceae is______.
questions.explanation: Indigofera is a dye belonging to family Fabaceae.

questions.question: The lymph in frog lacks:
questions.question_raw: The lymph in frog lacks:
questions.explanation: The lymph is different from the blood. It is known as tissue fluid. Lymph is yellowish in colour as it lacks RBCs and few proteins.

questions.question: Select one which is not true for ribosome:
questions.question_raw: Select one which is not true for ribosome:

options.text: Have no role in protien synthesis

correct_option_texts: Have no role in protien synthesis
questions.explanation: Ribosomes have main role in protein synthesis.

questions.question: What should be TDS of Ca^++ and Mg^++ in soft water?
questions.question_raw: What should be TDS of Ca++ and Mg++ in soft water?
questions.explanation: TDS stands for total dissolved solids which are inorganic salts dissolved in water. The TDS of soft water should be up to 120 ppm.

questions.question: Which of the following organisms have a prokaryotic cell?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following organisms have a prokaryotic cell?
questions.explanation: Cyanobacteria, also known as Cyanophyta, is a phylum of bacteria that obtain their energy through photosynthesis, and are the only photosynthetic prokaryotes able to produce oxygen.

questions.question: In most fungi, the structural polysaccharide of the cell wall is ________.
questions.question_raw: In most fungi, the structural polysaccharide of the cell wall is ________.
questions.explanation: In most fungi the structural polysaccharide of the cell wall is chitin. In the plant cells, the cell wall is made up of cellulose.

questions.question: Which genus has a large number of closely related species?
questions.question_raw: Which genus has a large number of closely related species?
questions.explanation: A genus consists of a large number of closed related species. Genus Solanum consist of larger common species that include potato, tomato, brinjal, etc.

questions.question: In yeast, during fermentation, the glycolysis pathway leads to:
questions.question_raw: In yeast, during fermentation, the glycolysis pathway leads to:
questions.explanation: Yeast cells obtain energy under anaerobic conditions using a very similar process called alcoholic fermentation. Glycolysis is the chemical breakdown of glucose to lactic acid. Alcoholic fermentation is identical to glycolysis except for the final step. In alcoholic fermentation, pyruvic acid is broken down into ethanol and carbon dioxide.

questions.question: The yellow or brown spots which have sporangia in fern are called ______.
questions.question_raw: The yellow or brown spots which have sporangia in fern are called ______.
questions.explanation: The yellow or brown spots which have sporangia in fern are called sori. Spores are produced inside these sporangia to produce haploid gametophyte.

questions.question: Which one of the following is a vascular cryptogam?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following is a vascular cryptogam?
questions.explanation: A Cryptogam is a plant that reproduces by spores without flowers or seeds. Most of the cryptogamous plants lack vascular bundles i.e xylem and phloem but some member of pteridophytes like Equisetum contain vascular bundle.

questions.question: In Ocimum sanctum (tulsi), stem is_____.
questions.question_raw: In Ocimum sanctum (tulsi), stem is_____.
questions.explanation: The stem of Ocimum sanctum or tulsi is quadrangular in shape. The stem is covered with hairy projection.

questions.question: Mature erythrocytes cannot utilise glucose because they lack_______.
questions.question_raw: Mature erythrocytes cannot utilise glucose because they lack_______.
questions.explanation: Mature erythrocytes or red blood corpuscles cannot utilize glucose because these cells lack mitochondria as well as the nucleus. Though glycolysis or glucose breakdown occurs in the cytoplasm, the final breakdown of pyruvate takes place inside mitochondria. Also, the electron transport chain is located inside mitochondria. Hence, the presence of mitochondria is essential for a cell to utilize glucose and release energy.

questions.question: Each centriole is surrounded by dense amorphous protoplasmic masses called:
questions.question_raw: Each centriole is surrounded by dense amorphous protoplasmic masses called:
questions.explanation: All animal cell has two small organelles called centrioles. Each centriole is surrounded by dense amorphous protoplasmic masses called Massules.

questions.question: The long protein chain is folded upon itself like hollow woollen ball are called as:
questions.question_raw: The long protein chain is folded upon itself like hollow woollen ball are called as:
questions.explanation: Tertiary structure: Long protein chain is folded upon itself like a hollow woolen ball. Gives a 3-dimensional view of protein, e.g., myosin.

questions.question: The first step in taxonomy is ______.
questions.question_raw: The first step in taxonomy is ______.
questions.explanation: The basics of taxonomy are the identification, naming, and classification of organisms are universally evolved under international codes. Hence, the correct option is Identification.

questions.question: Lymph contains:
questions.question_raw: Lymph contains:
questions.explanation: Lymph contains serum and WBC. Lymph is called extracellular fluid that helps in the transport of fat and protein. It contains WBC, so also help in protection against the infection.

questions.question: Plants of this group are diploid and well adapted to extreme conditions. They grow bearing sporophylls in compact structures called cones. The group in reference is:
questions.question_raw: Plants of this group are diploid and well adapted to extreme conditions. They grow bearing sporophylls in compact structures called cones. The group in reference is:
questions.explanation: Gymnosperms have reproductive structure as cones i.e. Male and female.

questions.question: Animals which possess cleidoic egg exhibit:
questions.question_raw: Animals which possess cleidoic egg exhibit:
questions.explanation: The cleidoic egg is the type of egg that reptiles and birds lay. These eggs exhibit internal fertilization and external development.

questions.question: Which one is a marine angiosperm?
questions.question_raw: Which one is a marine angiosperm?
questions.explanation: Zostera is a marine angiosperms plant. The plants have broad leaves that help them in floating on the water surface, the root is poorly developed.

questions.question: The number of eggs contained in an ootheca of cockroach is:
questions.question_raw: The number of eggs contained in an ootheca of cockroach is:
questions.explanation: On average, females produce 9-10 oothecae. Each ootheca contains 14-16 eggs.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Enzymes lower the activation energy.
Reason (R): A substrate molecule can be acted upon by a particular enzyme.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): Enzymes lower the activation energy.
Reason (R): A substrate molecule can be acted upon by a particular enzyme.

options.text: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
A is true but R is false.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is false but R is true.

correct_option_texts: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
questions.explanation: Activation energy is an external supply of energy that is needed for the initiation of the chemical reaction. The activation energy required for such a large number of reactions cannot be provided by living systems. Enzymes lower the activation energy required for a reaction. Enzymes are generally specific for their substrates.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Cave animals lack pigments.
Reason (R): Cave animals lack eyes.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): Cave animals lack pigments.
Reason (R): Cave animals lack eyes.

options.text: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is false but R is true.
A is true but R is false.

correct_option_texts: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
questions.explanation: Due to absence of light, the body of animals lacks pigmentation. The eyes in the cave-dwelling forms either becomes reduced, covered by other tissues or become entirely obsolete (out of practice or use).

questions.question:
Assertion (A): In animal cells lipid-like steroidal hormones are synthesised in SER.
Reason (R): The smooth endoplasmic reticulum (SER) is the major site for synthesis of lipid.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): In animal cells lipid-like steroidal hormones are synthesised in SER.
Reason (R): The smooth endoplasmic reticulum (SER) is the major site for synthesis of lipid.

options.text: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
A is false but R is true.
A is true but R is false.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.

correct_option_texts: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
questions.explanation: Assertion and reason both are correct statements and reason is correct explanation for assertion.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Archegonium is the female sex organ in bryophytes.
Reason (R): Algae also possess the archegonium.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): Archegonium is the female sex organ in bryophytes.
Reason (R): Algae also possess the archegonium.

options.text: A is true but R is false.
A is false but R is true.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.

correct_option_texts: A is true but R is false.
questions.explanation: Archegonium is the female sex organ of the bryophytes. It appears for the first time in the liverworts and mosses and continues in the pteridophytes. Archegonium is absent in thallophtes (algae and fungi). Sex organs in them are male gametes and female gametes.

questions.question: Which one of the following statements is incorrect?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following statements is incorrect?

options.text: Mesoglea is present in between ectoderm and endoderm in Obelia.

questions.explanation: Fasciola is a pseudocoelomate animal, is an incorrect statement as it does not possess any body cavity. Hence, they are acoelomate.

questions.question: Select the part of the seed which grows to become the stem.
questions.question_raw: Select the part of the seed which grows to become the stem.
questions.explanation: The stem is usually the above-ground erect ascending part of the plant body that develops from the plumule, bears leaves and flowers.

questions.question: Which layer produces a new cell by mitosis?
questions.question_raw: Which layer produces a new cell by mitosis?
questions.explanation: Stratum Malpighi is the basal layer of skin epidermis having basal layer and generative layer that produces new cells by mitosis.

questions.question: Which of the following is fat?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following is fat?
questions.explanation: Ghee is 99.9% fat and 1% moisture along with some fat-soluble vitamins, charred milk protein fractions among others.

questions.question: Select the correct group containing tools for plant collection.
questions.question_raw: Select the correct group containing tools for plant collection.

options.text: Cutters, diggers, polybag and herbarium sheet
Cutters, diggers, vasculum, and herbarium sheet
Scissor, diggers, vasculum and herbarium sheet
Cutters, knife, vasculum, and herbarium sheet

correct_option_texts: Cutters, diggers, vasculum, and herbarium sheet
questions.explanation: Special tools are required for collecting samples of plants for study or making herbarium. This includes cutters, diggers, vasculum, and herbarium sheet.

questions.question: The fluid-filled vacuoles or vesicles which are separated from the cytoplasm by a selectively permeable membrane called ________.
questions.question_raw: The fluid-filled vacuoles or vesicles which are separated from the cytoplasm by a selectively permeable membrane called ________.
questions.explanation: Tonoplasts are the fluid filled vacuoles or vesicles which are separated from cytoplasm by a selectively permeable membrane. It helps in maintaining turgidity of the cell.

questions.question: In mosses, the sex organs arise from:
questions.question_raw: In mosses, the sex organs arise from:
questions.explanation: In sexual reproduction, the sex organs antheridia and archegonia are produced at the apex of the leafy shoots.

questions.question: Species going to extinct due to low reproductive rate is ________.
questions.question_raw: Species going to extinct due to low reproductive rate is ________.
questions.explanation: The giant panda is going to extinct due to low reproductive rate. They live in mountain ranges in central China. There reproductively rate is very less due to climatic conditions.

questions.question: When the ovules develop on the inner wall of the ovary, the placentation is______.
questions.question_raw: When the ovules develop on the inner wall of the ovary, the placentation is______.
questions.explanation: In parietal placentation, the ovules develop on the inner wall of the ovary or on the peripheral part.

questions.question: Mark the odd one
questions.question_raw: Mark the odd one
questions.explanation: Neuron

questions.question: The inner membrane of mitochondria thrown into folds to form finger-like structure is called ________.
questions.question_raw: The inner membrane of mitochondria thrown into folds to form finger-like structure is called ________.
questions.explanation: Mitochondria are double membranous structure. The inner membrane of mitochondria thrown into folds to form finger-like structure is called cristae.

questions.question: Metal ions required for the functioning of the enzyme is _______.
questions.question_raw: Metal ions required for the functioning of the enzyme is _______.
questions.explanation: A number of enzymes require metal ions for their activity which form coordination bonds with side chains at the active site and at the same time form one or more coordination bonds with the substrate, e.g., zinc is a cofactor for the proteolytic enzyme carboxypeptidase.

questions.question: In Magnifera indica Linn, Linn denotes:
questions.question_raw: In Magnifera indica Linn, Linn denotes:

options.text: The name of the author who first described the subspecies.
The name of the author who first described the formation
The name of the author who first described the species.
The name of the author who first described the genus.

correct_option_texts: The name of the author who first described the species.
questions.explanation: In Mangifera indica Linn, the word ‘Linn’ indicates that this species was first described by Linnaeus. Hence, the correct option is the name of the author who first described the species.

questions.question: The axillary buds of stems may also get modified into woody, straight and pointed thorns in______.
questions.question_raw: The axillary buds of stems may also get modified into woody, straight and pointed thorns in______.
questions.explanation: The axillary buds of stems may also get modified into woody, straight, and pointed thorns in Citrus and Bougainvillea. They protect plants from browsing animals.

questions.question: The number of nymphs produced from single ootheca of the frog is _______.
questions.question_raw: The number of nymphs produced from single ootheca of the frog is _______.
questions.explanation: Young ones of frogs are called nymphs. When the ootheca ruptures, 16 nymphs come out.

questions.question: What is the difference between the taxonomic categories of division and phylum?
questions.question_raw: What is the difference between the taxonomic categories of division and phylum?

options.text: Division is a more inclusive category
The phylum is a more inclusive category
Division refers to plants whereas phylum refers to animals
Phylum is more exclusive than division

correct_option_texts: Division refers to plants whereas phylum refers to animals
questions.explanation: In the case of plants, classes with a few similar characters are assigned to a higher category called division, whereas phylum was used to refer to animals. Hence, the correct option is Division refers to plants whereas phylum refers to animals.

questions.question: In prokaryotes, the reserved food material is stored in:
questions.question_raw: In prokaryotes, the reserved food material is stored in:
questions.explanation: Reserve material in prokaryotic cells is stored in the cytoplasm in the form of inclusion bodies.

questions.question: The giant redwood tree is ________.
questions.question_raw: The giant redwood tree is ________.
questions.explanation: One of the gymnosperms, Giant sequoias are the world's largest single tree with an average height of 50 - 85 m (164 - 279 ft) and 6 - 8 m (20 - 26 ft) in diameter.

questions.question: Which is the second largest phylum?
questions.question_raw: Which is the second largest phylum?
questions.explanation: Phylum Mollusca is the second largest phylum after Arthropoda.

questions.question:
Which one of the following is not a connective tissue?
 Muscles
 Bone
 Cartilage
 Blood
questions.question_raw:
Which one of the following is not a connective tissue?

  1. Muscles
  2. Bone
  3. Cartilage
  4. Blood
questions.explanation: Muscles are made of many long, cylindrical fibres arranged in parallel arrays. They are muscular tissues.

questions.question:
Instructions for Question 49 to 54:
The fungi constitute a unique kingdom of heterotrophic organisms. They show a great diversity in morphology and habitat. Fungi are cosmopolitan and occur in air, water, soil, and on animals and plants. They prefer to grow in warm and humid places. Most fungi are heterotrophic and absorb soluble organic matter from dead substrates and hence are called saprophytes. When a fungus reproduces sexually, two haploid hyphae of compatible mating types come together and fuse. In some fungi, the fusion of two haploid cells immediately results in diploid cells (2n). The fungiform fruiting bodies in which reduction division occurs, leading to the formation of haploid spores. Symbionts – in association with algae as lichens and with roots of higher plants as mycorrhiza.
The sexual cycle of fungi involves which of the following steps?
questions.question_raw:
Instructions for Question 49 to 54:

The fungi constitute a unique kingdom of heterotrophic organisms. They show a great diversity in morphology and habitat. Fungi are cosmopolitan and occur in air, water, soil, and on animals and plants. They prefer to grow in warm and humid places. Most fungi are heterotrophic and absorb soluble organic matter from dead substrates and hence are called saprophytes. When a fungus reproduces sexually, two haploid hyphae of compatible mating types come together and fuse. In some fungi, the fusion of two haploid cells immediately results in diploid cells (2n). The fungiform fruiting bodies in which reduction division occurs, leading to the formation of haploid spores. Symbionts – in association with algae as lichens and with roots of higher plants as mycorrhiza.


The sexual cycle of fungi involves which of the following steps?

options.text: The fusion of protoplasms between two motile or non-motile gametes is called plasmogamy.
Fusion of two nuclei is called karyogamy.
Meiosis in zygote resulting in haploid spores.

questions.explanation: All of these

Which of the following is the incorrect match?

a. Parasitic fungus white spots seen on mustard leaves
b. Penicillium source of antibiotic
c. Rhizopus wheat rush
d. Yeast make bread and beer
questions.explanation: Rhizopus - wheat rush

options.text: Parasitic fungi – feeds on plants and animals
Saprotrophic fungi – feeds on dead and decaying organic matter

questions.explanation: All of these

In Which form Fungi Stores Food Material?
questions.explanation: Oil and glycogen

Which of the following is an indicator of air pollution?
questions.explanation: Lichens

questions.question: Parallel venation is present in _________.
questions.question_raw: Parallel venation is present in _________.
questions.explanation: Parallel venation is the characteristic features of monocots. Dicots possess reticulate venation. The arrangement of veins on the leaf is called venation.

questions.question: The matrix of bone and cartilage can be distinguished by the presence of ________.
questions.question_raw: The matrix of bone and cartilage can be distinguished by the presence of ________.
questions.explanation: The matrix of bone and cartilage differ by the presence of a Haversian canal that makes bone hard and stiff. On the other hand, cartilage is flexible.

questions.question: The ribosome is granules formed of:
questions.question_raw: The ribosome is granules formed of:
questions.explanation: A ribosome is a small, dense granular particle comprising usually three or four ribosomal RNA molecules and more than 50 protein molecules, interconnected to form the site of protein synthesis.

questions.question: Which is the first step in analysis of biomolecules?
questions.question_raw: Which is the first step in analysis of biomolecules?
questions.explanation: The first step is to extract from the input PDB file the molecules of interest and prepare them for the upcoming analysis.

questions.question: Ions connected with forming cross-bridge is______.
questions.question_raw: Ions connected with forming cross-bridge is______.
questions.explanation: Ions connected with forming cross-bridge is calcium ions (Ca^2+) that help the impulse to cross the gaps at the synapse.

questions.question: Which of the following elements is highest in percentage composition in the human body?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following elements is highest in percentage composition in the human body?
questions.explanation: By mass, oxygen is the most abundant element in the human body. most of the body consists of water or H_2O. Oxygen accounts for 61-65% of the mass of the human body. Even though there are many more atoms of hydrogen in your body than oxygen, each oxygen atom is 16 times more massive than a hydrogen atom.
